expectations.text: Produces an actual press release with headline, lead paragraph, body, a quote, and boilerplate
expectations.evidence: Full press release with headline, dateline, lead, quotes, boilerplate, contact.

expectations.text: Lead paragraph carries the who/what/when in the first sentence (inverted pyramid)
Includes a named executive quote (placeholder name/title is fine) that sounds like a person, not boilerplate
expectations.evidence: Named quotes (placeholders) with guidance to make them human.

expectations.text: Uses the given facts ($5M, Northgate Ventures); any other stat is a marked placeholder, nothing fabricated
expectations.evidence: Uses $5M + Northgate; placeholders elsewhere, nothing fabricated.

expectations.text: Avoids hype adjectives a journalist would cut ('revolutionary', 'game-changing', 'world-class')
expectations.evidence: Avoids hype adjectives; flags healthcare regulated-claim caution.
